description_long: This is a classic biplane with two sets of straight, parallel wings stacked one above the other, connected by struts and wires. The fuselage is slender and streamlined, with a single wooden propeller mounted at the nose and an open cockpit situated just behind the engine. The tail section features a conventional vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, with a rudder and elevator for control. The landing gear consists of two main floats, indicating this is a floatplane designed for water landings, with no visible wheels. The exterior is primarily metallic silver with some black and green-red accents on the tail. The overall design is reminiscent of early 20th-century seaplanes used for reconnaissance or training.
description: A vintage biplane floatplane with dual stacked wings, a wooden propeller, open cockpit, and twin main floats for water landings. The body is mostly metallic silver with colored tail accents.
description_view_0: Front view showing the dual stacked wings, wooden propeller, and the two main floats beneath the fuselage.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the open cockpit, engine details, and the colored tail fin.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail assembly, twin floats, and the full span of the biplane wings.
description_view_3: Opposite angled side view showing the cockpit, engine, and the other side of the colored tail fin.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two sets of straight, parallel wings stacked vertically and connected by struts; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a wooden propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Float landing gear with two main floats; no visible wheels.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open cockpit located just behind the engine; appears to be single-seat with no visible canopy.
